chunk_summary: Guidance on implementing feeding safety plans for a 9-year-old with EDACS III in a school setting, including IDDSI-based texture modifications, supervision, and emergency choking response aligned with AHA guidelines.
learning_objectives: Understand the application of IDDSI standards for modified textures in school settings.
Develop a comprehensive feeding safety plan for children with neurodisability and EDACS III.
Identify stop criteria and emergency response protocols for choking in schools.
Recognize the importance of consistent feeding practices to prevent aspiration and fatigue-related deterioration.
key_facts: The child has EDACS III, requiring 1:1 trained supervision during meals.
IDDSI codes must replace vague terms like 'thick liquids' for clarity and consistency.
Stop criteria include wet voice, persistent cough, choking, distress, drowsiness, or increased work of breathing.
Feeding during transport is not allowed to minimize aspiration risk.
Meal duration should have a hard maximum to prevent fatigue-related deterioration.
Emergency choking response must align with AHA guidelines, and trained personnel should be specified.
reference_quote: "Treat this as a safety systems problem: the child needs the same method every day, by every feeder, in every setting."
chunk_heading: NEURODISABILITY & FEEDING SUPPORT;CASE-BASED APPLICATION SCHOOL IMPLEMENTATION OF MODIFIED TEXTURES AND CHOKING RESPONSE;
